title: Chelsea teen charged with murder after allegedly chasing down, stabbing man
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Sergio Josue Castellanos Cerrato, 18, charged with murder after allegedly stabbing Eduardo Alfredo Rosales Lopez, 25, in Chelsea.
The incident occurred on March 19; police found multiple stabbing victims and tracked down the suspect shortly after.
Castellanos Cerrato pleaded not guilty and is held without bail until his next court appearance on April 21.

title: 2025 marked Boston area’s slowest year of post-pandemic population growth, data shows
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston metro area saw a population increase of only 11,991 from 2024 to 2025, the slowest since 2021.
Data shows Massachusetts' overall population growth in 2025 was just 15,524, a significant drop from previous years.
Middlesex County had the largest growth increase of 6,050, lower than the 19,736 recorded in 2024.

title: North Atlantic right whales now have a GoFundMe
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Cape Cod's Marineland Right Whale Project started a GoFundMe to raise $15,000 for North Atlantic right whale conservation.
The fundraiser supports research by Associated Scientists at Woods Hole, focusing on surveys and purchasing specialized equipment.
Only a few hundred North Atlantic right whales remain, threatened by vessel strikes and fishing gear entanglements.

title: 5 times the Patriots traded for wide receivers in recent history, and how each acquisition panned out
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Patriots acquired Randy Moss from the Raiders in 2007, leading to an NFL record for touchdowns in a single season.
Wes Welker joined the Patriots from the Dolphins in 2007, becoming a key slot receiver but never won a Super Bowl.
Josh Gordon was acquired in 2018, showing potential but ultimately failing to stay due to off-field issues.

title: Alex Cora not harping on late-game ejection by C.B. Bucknor: ‘We’re all human’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox manager Alex Cora was ejected in the 8th inning for arguing a check swing call against Trevor Story.
Umpire C.B. Bucknor did not defer the check swing call to the first base umpire, resulting in controversy.
Cora emphasized the importance of learning from the situation and managing the ABS challenge system better.

title: Trevor Story reacts to strike three call that got Alex Cora ejected in Red Sox’ loss
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Trevor Story disagreed with umpire CB Bucknor's strike three call, prompting manager Alex Cora's ejection during the Red Sox loss.
Story, who hit the Red Sox's first home run of 2026, was upset Bucknor didn't consult the first base umpire.
Boston tied the game with a late home run, but ultimately lost 6-5 in the 11th inning to start the season 1-1.

title: Brimming with motivation after missing Olympics, Pavel Zacha is playing his best hockey for Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Pavel Zacha scores 11 goals in 16 games since returning from Olympic break for Boston Bruins.
An injury kept Zacha from joining Czechia at the Milano-Cortina Olympics, leading to a strong NHL performance.
Zacha's 26 goals this season mark a career-high as Bruins approach playoff contention with nine games remaining.

title: Here’s what Red Sox SP Sonny Gray said about his rough debut
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Sonny Gray allowed four runs in four innings during his Red Sox debut against the Cincinnati Reds.
Manager Alex Cora noted Gray settled after a rough start but emphasized better defense was needed.
Boston's bullpen pitched well, but Red Sox lost 6-5 in 11 innings to the Reds.

title: Tens of thousands rally at Boston Common for third ‘No Kings’ protest
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Tens of thousands attended the third 'No Kings' rally in Boston Common, organized by ACLU of Massachusetts and others.
Speakers, including Gov. Maura Healey and Sen. Elizabeth Warren, addressed political threats and emphasized civic engagement.
Over 3,300 rallies occurred nationwide, with Massachusetts hosting over 160, highlighting widespread participation in the protest movement.

title: Review & setlist: GWAR gives fanbase a bloody good time at House of Blues
author: Christopher Hurley
bullets: GWAR performed a 90-minute set with 17 songs at the House of Blues in Boston.
The concert featured theatrical elements with fake blood, costumes, and satirical humor.
An effigy of Bill Clinton appeared on stage during the encore, ending with a rendition of 'Sick of You.'
